name: Oktapodi Krasato (Octopus in Red Wine)
recipeInstructions:
1. First clean the octopus. Pull off the tentacles, remove and discard the intestines, ink sac, eyes, beak, skin the octopus, wash thoroughly to remove any sand.
2. Cut into 1 1/2 to 2 inch pieces and put in a saucepan over medium heat. Stir until liquid evaporates.
3. Pour on the oil and stir to seal all sides. Add shallots and cook stirring once or twice until they lightly color.
4. Add wine, vinegar, tomatoes (chopped), tomato puree, bay leaves, oregano, and black pepper. Stir well, cover pan, and simmer gently for 60-75 minutes.
5. Check sauce occasionally to ensure it hasn't dried; add wine or water if needed. Octopus is done when easily pierced with a skewer.
6. Sauce should be thick like a runny paste; remove lid slightly increase heat and stir until some liquid evaporates and sauce thickens.
7. Discard bay leaves, stir in parsley, taste and adjust seasoning as necessary.
8. Serve with rice and salad or country bread for mopping up sauce.
